summary_text: **Findings (concise, ordered by severity)** - **High: runtime does not enforce fixed-slot posting at all.** Due selection accepts any approved `scheduled_for` within lateness window; there is no `POST_SCHEDULE` gate in `_find_due_post`. Ref: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L192), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L218), [config.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/config.py#L11) - **High: operator-managed schedule is inferred implicitly and silently, not explicit.** `backfill_today_slots` treats any non-canonical time as “operator-managed” and just skips backfill with INFO log. This hides policy drift from health/CI unless someone reads logs. Ref: [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L374), [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L383), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L489) - **High: health prints today’s schedule but does not fail/warn on off-slot approved posts.** Current health checks only missing/invalid time, low queue, etc. Off-slot drift is not classified. Ref: [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L189), [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L221) - **Medium: apply/preflight gates catch collisions and daily overload, but not slot validity policy.** `prepare_post_bundle` flags duplicate slot and overload, but not “non-fixed-slot without explicit override.” Ref: [prepare_post_bundle.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/prepare_post_bundle.py#L113), [prepare_post_bundle.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/prepare_post_bundle.py#L121), [prepare_post_bundle.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/prepare_post_bundle.py#L129) - **Medium: CI still excludes the broad scheduler suite where many schedule-policy regressions live.** `test_scheduler_r11_r12.py` is ignored in CI. Ref: [ci.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/.github/workflows/ci.yml#L68) - **Low: existing tests validate “manual non-slot => backfill skip,” which normalizes silent drift behavior.** Useful test, but it encodes implicit behavior instead of explicit policy contract. Ref: [test_backfill_today_slots.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_backfill_today_slots.py#L29) --- **Best concrete implementation (recommended)** 1. **Introduce explicit schedule policy in data model, not inference.** Add `schedule_mode` enum to `PostRecord` and Notion mapping: - `fixed_slots` (default) - `operator_override` Optional `schedule_override_reason` string. 2. **Add a shared slot validator and drift detector.** New helper in `post_generator.py`: - `is_valid_slot_for_day(scheduled_for, day)` using `POST_SCHEDULE` + Monday/Thursday note exception. - `detect_schedule_drift(posts, day)` returning off-slot approved rows and whether override is explicit. 3. **Runtime policy in scheduler (explicit, not silent).** In `_post_due_jobs` before backfill/posting: - If off-slot approved row exists with `schedule_mode != operator_override`: - emit `_send_alert("slot drift ...")` - skip auto-backfill/reslot - optionally quarantine to `failed` only if strict mode env is enabled. - If `operator_override` exists, log once as explicit override and continue. 4. **Health policy (fail-fast).** In `check_queue_health`: - detect today’s approved off-slot rows; - `FAIL` when unapproved override; - `WARN` when explicit `operator_override` is active (with count + titles). 5. **Preflight/apply policy.** In `prepare_post_bundle` and `apply_post_package --approve`: - block approving non-slot schedule unless `schedule_mode=operator_override` and reason present. 6. **CI policy split.** Keep fast lane, but add required slot-policy tests in main PR lane and move heavy scheduler suite to nightly if needed. Minimum required on PR: new slot validator/drift tests + health slot drift test + prepare/apply blocker tests. --- **Test plan (minimum set)** - `tests/test_slot_policy.py` (new) - fi...
